text.title: chunking-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for chunking-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Splitting mid-sentence and losing meaning at boundaries.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Abrupt sentence breaks create chunks that start or end mid-thought, confusing embedding models and reducing retrieval relevance.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Queries spanning chunk boundaries may fail to retrieve any relevant results. Overlap acts as a context bridge.

failure-mode.description: Using the same chunk size for all document types.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Code, legal documents, and news articles have different optimal sizes. One-size-fits-all approaches sacrifice quality across all domains.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fragments lose surrounding context and may not contain enough information to answer questions independently.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Dilutes relevance. Embedding models struggle with long texts. LLMs waste context budget on irrelevant sections.
